definitions.archive: Directly inspectable research record that does not currently clear all public evidence gates. It receives no headline evidence credit.
definitions.calibration: Strict registration and public method gate passed, but the design is associational, descriptive, canonical-case, or screening-grade.
definitions.estimator_floor: Directional verdicts cannot receive public evidence credit when the primary estimator failed, was rank-deficient, fell back, produced an effectively-zero coefficient, or could not estimate uncertainty.
definitions.featured: Strict spec-before-run registration; public method gate; estimator floor passed; causal evidence type; no screening markers.
definitions.reference_set: Small hand-audited set of useful exemplars. Membership is not peer review and does not override the evidence tier.

records.reference_note: Long-horizon causal-design example with a supported directional result.
Fiscal credibility example selected to expose estimator and threshold details.
Long-run transition example with a supported result and strict registration.
Refuted causal-design example; included to make adverse results prominent.
Trade event example with a supported result and inspectable replication.
Refuted welfare-policy example; included to balance topics and verdict direction.
